item.title: Blackstone Forms New Unit for AI and Tech Bets; Korngold to Exit
item.description: Blackstone Inc. is folding its growth business into a new West Coast-based division that will focus solely on the alternative investment giant’s artificial intelligence portfolio, which includes OpenAI and Anthropic PBC.

item.title: OpenAI Sued in US by Families of Canada School Shooting Victims
item.description: OpenAI is the target of new lawsuits over the mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, that allege the artificial intelligence company could have stopped the suspected killer from using its popular chatbot, ChatGPT, ahead of the attack.

item.title: Why Humanoid Robots Are the Ultimate AI Frontier
item.description: On this episode of Bloomberg Primer, we explore the gap between futuristic hype and reality—and how quickly it will shrink.

item.title: EQT Is Said to Prepare Improved Takeover Offer for UK’s Intertek
item.description: EQT AB is preparing to make another improved offer for Intertek Group Plc, people familiar with the matter said, after seeing two bids rejected by the British product testing company.

item.title: Citi Picks Veteran Mideast Banker Tannir for Top Regional Role
item.description: Citigroup Inc. has hired Karim Tannir, one of the most senior investment bankers in the Gulf, to lead its Middle East and Africa business as part of its efforts to make deeper inroads into a lucrative region.

item.title: Ackman’s $5 Billion Pershing IPO Squeaks Across the Finish Line
item.description: Bill Ackman’s Pershing Square is finally going public after years of social media-hyping about building an empire modeled on Warren Buffett’s, and even after raising $5 billion for his vision, the hedge-fund billionaire faces another test.

item.title: US Core Capital Goods Orders Surge by Most Since 2020
item.description: US orders for business equipment increased in March by the most since mid-2020, extending a yearlong stretch of solid capital investment fueled by spending on artificial intelligence.

item.title: US Housing Starts Surge to Highest Level Since December 2024
item.description: New residential construction in the US rose in March to the highest in more than a year as homebuilders boosted production despite ongoing affordability concerns.

item.title: Kalshi, Polymarket Face New Rival in Crypto’s Hottest Exchange
item.description: Hyperliquid, the decentralized crypto exchange that has emerged as one of the most active trading venues in digital assets, is proposing to add prediction markets to its platform — a direct challenge to Kalshi and Polymarket as the fast-growing sector draws new competitors.

item.title: Cambricon’s Revenue Jumps on Strong AI Chip Demand in China
item.description: Chinese AI chipmaker Cambricon Technologies Corp.’s first-quarter sales more than doubled, as Beijing’s push for self-sufficiency in critical technologies including semiconductors has fueled local demand for its products.

item.title: Defense Upstarts Capitalize on Global Conflict as Budgets Shift
item.description: A new crop of defense startups from drone makers to software developers are emerging on Wall Street as governments raise military budgets and refill depleting supplies amid intensifying geopolitical tensions.

item.title: Euro Area Sentiment Falls as Confidence Weakens Across Sectors
item.description: Euro area economic sentiment fell to 93 in April from 96.2 in March, marking a third consecutive monthly decline, European Commission data showed Wednesday.

item.title: Ukraine Says It Hit Russia’s Oil Station and Sanctioned Tanker
item.description: Ukraine said it hit an oil pumping station deep inside Russia, as well as a sanctioned tanker in the Black Sea, marking the latest strikes on its adversary’s energy infrastructure.

item.title: Wingstop Sinks as Fewer Diners Feasted on Fast-Food Chicken
item.description: Wingstop Inc. shares fell after the company lowered its full-year guidance and reported worst-than-expected results, confirming concerns that traffic to the chicken chain had significantly dropped off.

item.title: German Inflation Up Less Than Expected as ECB Mulls War Response
item.description: German inflation accelerated less than anticipated, supporting the European Central Bank’s arguments to defer a decision on raising interest rates due to the Iran war.

item.title: America’s Bond-Market Privilege Is Disappearing as US Debt Soars
item.description: For much of the past few years, US Treasuries have failed to serve their traditional role as a sure-fire refuge from global market meltdowns.

item.title: GE HealthCare Drops After Cutting Outlook on War-Related Woes
item.description: GE HealthCare Technologies Inc. shares sank after the company reported a profit miss and cut its outlook, citing supply issues and higher costs.

item.title: Trump’s Goal of Reshoring Comes Up Short After a Year of Tariffs
item.description: A new Kearney report shows reviving US factory output could be a long slog

item.title: US Premarket Movers for April 29, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures are little changed as of 7:45 a.m. in New York ahead of a blockbuster slate of corporate earnings. Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon and Meta Platforms are set to report after the close.

item.title: UAE’s OPEC Split Reflects Twilight of the Oil Age
item.description: Abu Dhabi’s departure suggests an urgency to open the taps while it still can.

item.title: European Sugar Rift Deepens Over Plan to Curb Duty-Free Imports
item.description: The European Commission’s plan to curb some duty-free sugar imports to bolster prices is pitting beet growers against cane refiners and multinational food giants, in a dispute that risks undermining the region’s competitiveness.

item.title: US Stock Futures Edge Up on Tech Gains Before Megacap Earnings
item.description: US equity futures advanced on Wednesday as tech stocks climbed ahead of the earnings results from four megacap companies later in the day.

item.title: Cosco’s Profit Drops by Half as Lower Freight Rates Bite
item.description: Cosco Shipping Holdings Co. reported a 50% drop in first-quarter profit on weaker freight rates, warning that war in the Middle East has posed “significant challenges” for the industry.

item.title: Russia Says It Has No Plans to Leave OPEC+ After UAE Exit
item.description: Russia said it has no plans of leaving its alliance with OPEC after the United Arab Emirates’ shock decision to quit the cartel raised questions about its future amid a historic supply disruption caused by the Iran war.

item.title: China’s Shenzhen Relaxes Home Buying Rules to Boost Demand
item.description: China’s tech hub of Shenzhen will ease home buying restrictions in the city’s prime districts and raise housing provident fund loan caps in the latest attempt by authorities to contain the nation’s prolonged property slump.

item.title: Chiesi Agrees to Buy KalVista Pharmaceuticals for $1.9 Billion
item.description: Chiesi Farmaceutici SpA agreed to acquire US-listed KalVista Pharmaceuticals Inc. for about $1.9 billion in its biggest acquisition to date, expanding the Italian company’s rare immunology portfolio.

item.title: The Manus Mess Won’t Dim Singapore’s Appeal for Startups
item.description: Chinese founders will keep coming to Singapore, even after Manus flashpoint

item.title: Blue Owl Shares Near ‘Doomsday’ Levels See Earnings as Next Risk
item.description: Blue Owl Capital Inc., whose battered stock has served as a proxy for concerns over the health of the $1.8 trillion private credit industry, will face fresh scrutiny from investors and Wall Street analysts with the release of its first-quarter earnings.

item.title: Why Trimmed-Mean US Inflation May Trim a Bit Too Much for the Fed
item.description: Fed nominee Kevin Warsh has praised “trimmed mean” inflation.

item.title: Crypto Firm MoonPay Bets $100 Million for Institutional Push
item.description: MoonPay Inc. has acquired Sodot, an Israeli crypto security startup, as part of a plan by the crypto payments firm to launch a new unit focused exclusively on catering to institutional demand for digital assets.
